title: nie-hlns W1B: Transport layer to JSON-RPC
description:
## What
Rewrite core/src/transport.rs to send JsonRpcRequest and receive JsonRpcResponse/JsonRpcNotification. Add a lightweight request-ID tracker. Keep the mpsc channel architecture; demux responses vs notifications in the read task.

## Algorithm
1. Add RequestTracker to transport (not public):
   - next_id: AtomicU64 (start at 1)
   - pending: DashMap<u64, oneshot::Sender<JsonRpcResponse>>
   fn next_id(&self) -> u64 { self.next_id.fetch_add(1, Ordering::SeqCst) }
   fn register(&self, id: u64) -> oneshot::Receiver<JsonRpcResponse> { ... }
   fn resolve(&self, response: JsonRpcResponse) { ... }  // looks up id, sends on oneshot
2. Rewrite open_authenticated_ws():
   a. Receive frame; parse as JsonRpcNotification; verify method==CHALLENGE; extract nonce
   b. Sign nonce; create AuthenticateParams { pub_key, nonce, signature }
   c. Generate id=1; create JsonRpcRequest { version:"2.0", id, method:AUTHENTICATE, params:... }
   d. Send JSON; wait for response (parse as JsonRpcResponse with matching id=1)
   e. If response.error → return Err(AuthFailed)
   f. If response.result → extract AuthenticateResult { pub_id, subscription_expires }; return Ok
3. Update reader task:
   a. Try deserialize frame as JsonRpcResponse (has 'result' OR 'error' field, has 'id')
   b. If it matches: call tracker.resolve(response); do NOT send on client_rx
   c. Else: try deserialize as JsonRpcNotification (has 'method', no 'id')
   d. If notification: convert to ClientEvent::Message(notification) and send on client_rx
   e. Unknown JSON: warn! and continue
4. Update send side (writer task):
   - Incoming from tx: already a JsonRpcRequest (type changes from RelayMessage to JsonRpcRequest)
   - Serialize to JSON string; send as text frame
5. Change RelayConn and RelayConnRetry public types:
   - tx: mpsc::Sender<JsonRpcRequest>  (was RelayMessage)
   - rx: mpsc::Receiver<ClientEvent>  (ClientEvent::Message now carries JsonRpcNotification, not RelayMessage)
6. Update ClientEvent:
   - Message(JsonRpcNotification)  (was RelayMessage)
   - Reconnecting { delay_secs: u64 }  (unchanged)
   - Reconnected  (unchanged)

## Defensive programming rules
- Never reuse a request id within a session (monotonic counter)
- If a response arrives for an unknown id: warn! and discard
- If the read task drops a pending request (disconnect): all pending oneshotsreceive Err (drop the sender)
- Auth failure must produce a descriptive Err, not a panic
- serde_json::from_str failures on notifications: warn! + continue (don't crash)
- No unwrap() on response parsing (can fail on malformed JSON)

## Agent team: Critical-path (R + I_A + I_B + T + DIFF)
- R: Read core/src/transport.rs in full; document current auth flow, channel types, reconnection manager
- I_A: Implement transport rewrite from R's summary
- I_B: Implement transport rewrite independently from R's summary
- T: Write unit tests for RequestTracker (ID generation, resolve, unknown ID); integration test auth flow with mock relay
- DIFF: Compare I_A and I_B; verify both handle the auth handshake identically

## Tests
- request_id_monotonic: generate 100 ids, assert each > previous
- response_routes_to_correct_waiter: register two ids, send responses out-of-order, verify each waiter gets its own response
- unknown_response_id_discarded: send response with unregistered id, verify no panic, client_rx stays clean
- notification_passes_through: receive JsonRpcNotification, verify it appears in client_rx
- auth_failure_propagates: mock relay sends error response to authenticate → connect() returns Err
acceptance_criteria: cargo build -p nie-core passes; RelayConn.tx type is mpsc::Sender<JsonRpcRequest>; auth handshake tests pass; clippy clean; no RelayMessage in transport.rs
